stem: A computer programmer has developed a computer game that they want to release for users to download over the internet. The programmer needs to decide whether to release the game as open source or proprietary software.
model_answer: A full band 3 answer covers: (1) features of each licence (open source is usually free, source code is available and can be modified and redistributed; proprietary is purchased, source code is hidden and cannot be altered); (2) legal/ethical points (both are copyrighted; open source exposes work to being adapted or rebadged; proprietary restricts what users can do but protects the developer's income); (3) benefits and drawbacks applied to the game (open source reaches a wider player base and lets the community fix bugs, but earns little money; proprietary gives financial return and tight quality control, but limits who can play or modify); (4) a justified recommendation that references the scenario — for example 'recommend proprietary because the programmer needs income from download sales, and for a game the user experience is tightly controlled'.
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q4 (QER, 8 marks — AO2.1a x4, AO2.1b x4). Mark by levels: L1 1-2, L2 3-5, L3 6-8. Expect features + legal/ethical + benefits/drawbacks + justified recommendation.

parts.label: (a)*
parts.prompt: Discuss the features, benefits and drawbacks of each type of licence for this program and make a recommendation to the programmer. Include: features of each licence; legal and ethical issues of each licence; benefits and drawbacks of each licence.

mark_points.text: Features of open source: free to download, source code is available, can be modified and redistributed
Features of proprietary: purchased at a cost, source code is hidden, modification/redistribution not allowed
Legal/ethical: both provide copyright protection; open source can be rebranded or resold by others; proprietary restricts users' rights
Open source benefit: wider audience, community can fix bugs / improve the game
Open source drawback: little direct financial gain, limited documentation, risk of altered versions confusing users
Proprietary benefit: income from sales, tight control over the user experience, usually better-tested releases
Proprietary drawback: smaller audience (paywall), cannot be adapted to individual user needs
Justified recommendation that explicitly references this scenario (the game and the programmer)

misconceptions.label: No recommendation
misconceptions.description: Answers that list pros/cons but do not conclude with a justified choice are capped at the middle band.

misconceptions.label: Open source = free of copyright
misconceptions.description: Open source software is still copyrighted; the licence grants specific freedoms.
